title: Quarto Pressmark
text:
Pressmark - an elegant, typographic theme for Quarto websites. Inspired by newspapers like The Atlantic and the Financial Times as well as Edward Tufte’s design principles. Made for sites centered around text, that do not want to compromise on good design. Feauturing carefully selected fonts, well thought-trough default styling, and a bunch of quirks.
GitHub Showcase

Quickstart
Learn more in the Setup Guide


Features








Banners and Table of Contents


A more prominent way to present author, title and date in the title block










Customization


Making Pressmark work for you (e.g with a Dark Mode)










Listings


Introduction to a very useful quarto feature










Typography


More about typographic quirks like Smallcaps and Drop Caps










Markup Reference


A showcase of what things look like with Pressmark










Citations


Some tips for academics using Quarto





No matching items


Pressmark noun: A notation or figure in the margin of a printed sheet indicating the press on which it was printed.
The American Heritage® Dictionary of the English Language, 5th Edition.

title: Markup Reference
text:
Footnotes


My reference.↩︎
Every new line should be prefixed with 2 spaces.
This allows you to have a footnote with multiple lines.↩︎
Named footnotes will still render with numbers instead of the text but allow easier identification and linking.
This footnote also has been made with a different syntax using 4 spaces for new lines.↩︎
